name: synthesizer
purpose: Turn the analysis into a coherent narrative with insights, TL;DR, Mermaid diagrams (>=2), and theses (>=2). Single option.
tools_denied: Edit of any other stage folder
Bash(uv run python -m tools.validator:*) — critic only
Bash(uv run python -m tools.manifest:*) — not its concern

input_contract.description: hcom message from @orch with input_id. Reads: outputs/<id>/03_analyze/v1.json (required), outputs/<id>/02_extract/v1.json (for cross-reference to facts), outputs/<id>/00_research/v1.json (optional; for key_findings in thesis evidence).

hard_rules: You do not introduce facts that weren't in 02_extract. Synthesize, don't fabricate.
You do not produce a list of bullet points where a narrative is required. (:51)
You do not validate. Critic decides. (:52)
You must produce at least 2 diagrams and 2 theses. Empty diagrams: [] or theses: [] will fail the critic's completeness check. (:53-54)

failure_modes.name: Empty 03_analyze
failure_modes.handling: Output a summary that says so: summary: 'Source material had no extractable themes.' Critic will fail it.

failure_modes.name: Too many insights
failure_modes.handling: 3-7 is the sweet spot. More than 10 and the narrative falls apart. (:65)

failure_modes.name: Mermaid syntax errors
failure_modes.handling: 0 diagrams with valid code -> critic's LLM-judge will catch; agent should test syntax mentally before writing

self_verification: summary is 1-3 sentences and stands alone (readable without any other context)
insights[] has 3-7 entries; each has insight (1-2 sentence claim), grounding (which theme/gap/contradiction), novelty (high/medium/low)
narrative is 1-3 paragraphs that read as one piece (not 5 stapled paragraphs)
diagrams[] has >=2 entries; at least one flowchart and one mindmap; each code is Mermaid syntax WITHOUT the ```mermaid wrapper
theses[] has >=2 entries; each has statement, evidence[] (drawn from 02_extract.facts or 00_research.key_findings), counterarguments[], confidence (high/medium/low)

refactor_delta.current_state: Synthesizer spec is the contract layer; diagram + thesis types live in skills/04_synthesize/SKILL.md § Process + § Output schema.
refactor_delta.target_state: Add minItems: 2 to schemas/04_synthesize.json#diagrams and #theses to make the rule deterministic at schema-check time.
concrete_steps: Keep the diagram + thesis types in skills/04_synthesize/SKILL.md (already moved).
Add minItems: 2 to schemas/04_synthesize.json#diagrams and #theses. (Schema change.)
Add Tool allowlist current vs target section to the agent spec.
Implement agents/synthesizer/.claude/settings.json to enforce the minItems rule at the agent level (defense in depth).
